name: Karrysalat (Curried Macaroni & Herring Salad)
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrape out and discard the seeds from the halved cucumber using a teaspoon. Slice the halves into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Place these cubes in a small bowl and sprinkle with salt to draw out their excess moisture. Let the cubes stand for 15 minutes, then drain and pat them dry with paper towels.
3. Cook the macaroni in rapidly boiling salted water for 20 minutes, following the package directions. Meanwhile, wash the herring fillets in cold water, pat them dry with paper towels, and cut into 1-1/2-inch lengths. Drain the cooked macaroni in a colander, run cold water over it to cool, then spread out on paper towels to remove excess moisture.
4. Place the cut-up herring in a salad bowl along with the cucumber, macaroni, and sliced raw mushrooms.
5. In a separate bowl, beat together the vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper with a small wire whisk and pour this mixture over the ingredients in the salad bowl. Mix the salad thoroughly but gently using two wooden spoons.
6. Refrigerate the salad for 1 to 2 hours before serving.
7. For a richer dressing preferred by the Danes, whip the mayonnaise, sour cream, and curry powder together in a small bowl and add to the salad just before serving.
